title: Evaluate Past Nowcasts Accuracy
text: can run evaluation parameters nowcast_cl().nowcast_eval() one additional parameter: n_past, controls many steps past wish run nowcast . return S7 object 2 slots:
code: library(nowcastr) #> Warning: replacing previous import 'DT::dataTableOutput' by #> 'shiny::dataTableOutput' when loading 'nowcastr' #> Warning: replacing previous import 'DT::renderDataTable' by #> 'shiny::renderDataTable' when loading 'nowcastr' nc_eval_obj <-   nowcast_demo %>%   nowcast_eval(     n_past = 10,     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     do_model_fitting = TRUE   ) #> Evaluating nowcast  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 5/10 | ETA:  1s #> Evaluating nowcast  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■     9/10 | ETA:  0s #> Evaluating nowcast  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  10/10 | ETA:  0s nc_eval_obj@detail %>% dplyr::glimpse(0) #> Rows: 958 #> Columns: 12 #> $ group            <chr> … #> $ cut_date         <date> … #> $ date_occurrence  <date> … #> $ last_r_date      <date> … #> $ value            <dbl> … #> $ value_predicted  <dbl> … #> $ value_true       <dbl> … #> $ delay            <dbl> … #> $ SAPE_pred        <dbl> … #> $ SAPE_obs         <dbl> … #> $ SAPE_improvement <dbl> … #> $ pred_is_better   <int> … nc_eval_obj@summary %>% dplyr::glimpse(0) #> Rows: 96 #> Columns: 14 #> $ group                     <chr> … #> $ delay                     <dbl> … #> $ n_periods                 <int> … #> $ n_obs                     <int> … #> $ SMAPE_pred                <dbl> … #> $ SMAPE_obs                 <dbl> … #> $ SMAPE_improvement_mean    <dbl> … #> $ SMAPE_improvement_med     <dbl> … #> $ SMAPE_improvement_q1      <dbl> … #> $ SMAPE_improvement_q3      <dbl> … #> $ proportion_pred_is_better <dbl> … #> $ n_pairs                   <int> … #> $ CI_lower                  <dbl> … #> $ CI_upper                  <dbl> …

title: Evaluate Past Nowcasts Accuracy
text: “SMAPE Improvement median” = median difference SMAPE(observed) SMAPE(predicted) “Proportion Better = proportion predictions outperform base values (-50% center around zero)
code: plot_nowcast_eval(nc_eval_obj, delay = 0) plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay(nc_eval_obj, indicator = "SMAPE_improvement_med")

title: Evaluate Past Nowcasts Accuracy
text: “SMAPE Improvement median” = median difference SMAPE(observed) SMAPE(predicted) “Proportion Better = proportion predictions outperform base values (-50% center around zero)
code: plot_nowcast_eval(nc_eval_obj, delay = 0)

what: Plot one indicator by delay / for one indicator
title: Evaluate Past Nowcasts Accuracy
code: plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay(nc_eval_obj, indicator = "SMAPE_improvement_med")

what: Plot raw values / for one delay
title: Evaluate Past Nowcasts Accuracy
text: predicted values base reported values, time last reported values
code: plot_nowcast_eval_detail(nc_eval_obj, delay = 0) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

title: Evaluate Past Nowcasts Accuracy
text: test accuracy nowcasts improve fill_future_reported_values():
code: library(nowcastr) nc_eval_obj_with_fill <-   nowcast_demo %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     max_delay = "auto"   ) %>%   nowcast_eval(     n_past = 10,     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     do_model_fitting = TRUE   ) #> Evaluating nowcast  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 5/10 | ETA:  1s #> Evaluating nowcast  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  10/10 | ETA:  0s plot_nowcast_eval(nc_eval_obj_with_fill, delay = 0)

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: package available GitHub. Install :
code: devtools::install_github("whocov/nowcastr") library(nowcastr) #> Warning: replacing previous import 'DT::dataTableOutput' by #> 'shiny::dataTableOutput' when loading 'nowcastr' #> Warning: replacing previous import 'DT::renderDataTable' by #> 'shiny::renderDataTable' when loading 'nowcastr'

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: dataset must contain least three columns: occurrence date: event happened reporting date: event reported value: observed count/value <groups>: none, one multiple grouping columns: e.g. group_cols = c("group") # c("region", "disease") package includes demo dataset nowcast_demo follows structure demo data also includes group column demonstrating grouped processing, though can multiple grouping columns.

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: typical nowcasting workflow nowcastr involves following steps. nowcasting, inspect reporting pattern data:  “millipede” plot provides alternative view delays:  may want fill missing values last known reporting values ensure consistent time units:  step optional; nowcast_cl can handle unfilled data. Perform nowcasting using chain-ladder method: nowcast_cl() function returns nowcast_results object containing predictions, delay distributions, completeness estimates, parameters. Access components result object: Plot results:   Open Shiny app explore results group group:
code: nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "millipede",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) data_filled <- nowcast_demo %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     max_delay = "auto"   ) data_filled %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) nc_obj <-   data_filled %>%   nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     do_model_fitting = TRUE   ) S7::prop_names(nc_obj) #>  [1] "name"         "params"       "time_start"   "time_end"     "n_groups"     #>  [6] "max_delay"    "data"         "completeness" "delays"       "models"       #> [11] "results" nc_obj@results # Final nowcasted values #> # A tibble: 95 × 7 #>    group    date_occurrence last_r_date delay value value_predicted completeness #>    <chr>    <date>          <date>      <dbl> <dbl>           <dbl>        <dbl> #>  1 SARS-Co… 2025-11-17      2025-11-17      0     0             0        0.00391 #>  2 SARS-Co… 2025-11-10      2025-11-17      1     0             0        0.0316  #>  3 SARS-Co… 2025-11-03      2025-11-17      2     1            11.4      0.0878  #>  4 SARS-Co… 2025-10-27      2025-11-17      3     6            36.5      0.164   #>  5 SARS-Co… 2025-10-20      2025-11-17      4     7            27.8      0.252   #>  6 SARS-Co… 2025-10-13      2025-11-17      5    21            61.5      0.341   #>  7 SARS-Co… 2025-10-06      2025-11-17      6    52           122.       0.427   #>  8 SARS-Co… 2025-09-29      2025-11-17      7    55           109.       0.507   #>  9 SARS-Co… 2025-09-22      2025-11-17      8    70           121.       0.577   #> 10 SARS-Co… 2025-09-15      2025-11-17      9    60            93.9      0.639   #> # ℹ 85 more rows nc_obj@delays # Delay distribution #> # A tibble: 96 × 5 #>    group                      delay     n completeness_obs completeness_modelled #>    <chr>                      <dbl> <int>            <dbl>                 <dbl> #>  1 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     0    10          0.0169                0.00391 #>  2 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     1    10          0.00670               0.0316  #>  3 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     2    10          0.0646                0.0878  #>  4 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     3    10          0.163                 0.164   #>  5 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     4    10          0.250                 0.252   #>  6 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     5    10          0.321                 0.341   #>  7 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     6    10          0.442                 0.427   #>  8 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     7    10          0.537                 0.507   #>  9 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     8    10          0.611                 0.577   #> 10 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     9    10          0.668                 0.639   #> # ℹ 86 more rows nc_obj@completeness # Data with completeness estimates #> # A tibble: 2,478 × 8 #>    group         date_occurrence date_report value delay last_value completeness #>    <chr>         <date>          <date>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>        <dbl> #>  1 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-17      2025-11-17      0     0          0        1     #>  2 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-10      2025-11-17      0     1          0        1     #>  3 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-10      2025-11-10      0     0          0        1     #>  4 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-03      2025-11-17      1     2          1        1     #>  5 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-03      2025-11-10      0     1          1        0     #>  6 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-03      2025-11-03      0     0          1        0     #>  7 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-27      2025-11-17      6     3          6        1     #>  8 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-27      2025-11-10      2     2          6        0.333 #>  9 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-27      2025-11-03      0     1          6        0     #> 10 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-20      2025-11-17      7     4          7        1     #> # ℹ 2,468 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: reportweight <dbl> str(nc_obj@params) # Parameters used #> List of 16 #>  $ col_date_occurrence         : chr "date_occurrence" #>  $ col_date_reporting          : chr "date_report" #>  $ col_value                   : chr "value" #>  $ group_cols                  : chr "group" #>  $ time_units                  : chr "weeks" #>  $ max_delay                   : NULL #>  $ max_reportunits             : num 10 #>  $ max_completeness            : num 5 #>  $ min_completeness_samples    : num 1 #>  $ use_weighted_method         : logi TRUE #>  $ do_propagate_missing_delays : logi FALSE #>  $ do_model_fitting            : logi TRUE #>  $ model_names                 : chr [1:6] "monomolecular" "vonbertalanffy" "logistic" "gompertz" ... #>  $ do_use_modelled_completeness: logi TRUE #>  $ rss_threshold               : num 0.01 #>  $ output                      : chr "all" plot(nc_obj, which = "delays") # Delay distribution plot(nc_obj, which = "results") # Nowcast time series explore_nowcast(nc_obj)

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: nowcasting, inspect reporting pattern data:  “millipede” plot provides alternative view delays:
code: nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "millipede",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: may want fill missing values last known reporting values ensure consistent time units:  step optional; nowcast_cl can handle unfilled data.
code: data_filled <- nowcast_demo %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     max_delay = "auto"   ) data_filled %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: Perform nowcasting using chain-ladder method: nowcast_cl() function returns nowcast_results object containing predictions, delay distributions, completeness estimates, parameters.
code: nc_obj <-   data_filled %>%   nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     do_model_fitting = TRUE   ) S7::prop_names(nc_obj) #>  [1] "name"         "params"       "time_start"   "time_end"     "n_groups"     #>  [6] "max_delay"    "data"         "completeness" "delays"       "models"       #> [11] "results"

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: chain-ladder method estimates “completeness” delay bucket: Delay = reporting date - occurrence date Completeness = observed value / last reported value (approximation true value) Average completeness per delay bucket (across occurrence dates) Nowcast = observed value / average completeness Recent occurrence dates shorter delays lower completeness. method upweights observations estimate true count. can nowcast multiple groups (e.g., regions, diseases) single call specifying multiple grouping columns:
code: nowcast_cl(   # ...   group_cols = c("region", "disease") )

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: can nowcast multiple groups (e.g., regions, diseases) single call specifying multiple grouping columns:
code: nowcast_cl(   # ...   group_cols = c("region", "disease") )

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: retro_score = number actual value changes / max possible value changes [0-1] opposite fill_future_reported_values(). can useful reduce data size without losing information.
code: # Calculate retro-scores (= number of actual value changes / max possible value changes) nowcast_demo %>%   calculate_retro_score(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = c("group")   ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   group                          n_changes max_retro_adj retro_score #>   <chr>                              <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 SARS-CoV-2 non-STL Positivity        385           575       0.670 #> 2 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions       374           575       0.650 #> 3 Syndromic ILI                        371           575       0.645 #> 4 Syndromic ARI                        299           575       0.52 # Remove duplicate reported values (same value and higher reporting date) nowcast_demo %>%   rm_repeated_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = c("group")   ) #> # A tibble: 1,624 × 4 #>    value date_occurrence date_report group                          #>    <dbl> <date>          <date>      <chr>                          #>  1    12 2024-12-16      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  2    31 2024-12-23      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  3    22 2024-12-30      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  4    21 2024-12-30      2025-06-02  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  5    18 2025-01-06      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  6    19 2025-01-06      2025-06-16  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  7    11 2025-01-13      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  8     7 2025-01-20      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  9     8 2025-01-20      2025-06-16  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #> 10    17 2025-01-27      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #> # ℹ 1,614 more rows

what: Calculate Retro Scores of input data
title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: retro_score = number actual value changes / max possible value changes [0-1]
code: # Calculate retro-scores (= number of actual value changes / max possible value changes) nowcast_demo %>%   calculate_retro_score(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = c("group")   ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   group                          n_changes max_retro_adj retro_score #>   <chr>                              <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 SARS-CoV-2 non-STL Positivity        385           575       0.670 #> 2 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions       374           575       0.650 #> 3 Syndromic ILI                        371           575       0.645 #> 4 Syndromic ARI                        299           575       0.52

title: Getting Started with nowcastr
text: opposite fill_future_reported_values(). can useful reduce data size without losing information.
code: # Remove duplicate reported values (same value and higher reporting date) nowcast_demo %>%   rm_repeated_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = c("group")   ) #> # A tibble: 1,624 × 4 #>    value date_occurrence date_report group                          #>    <dbl> <date>          <date>      <chr>                          #>  1    12 2024-12-16      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  2    31 2024-12-23      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  3    22 2024-12-30      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  4    21 2024-12-30      2025-06-02  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  5    18 2025-01-06      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  6    19 2025-01-06      2025-06-16  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  7    11 2025-01-13      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  8     7 2025-01-20      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  9     8 2025-01-20      2025-06-16  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #> 10    17 2025-01-27      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #> # ℹ 1,614 more rows

title: Authors and Citation
text: Mathias Leroy. Author, maintainer. Finlay Campbell. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Leroy M, Campbell F (2026). nowcastr: Nowcasting Chain-Ladder Method. R package version 0.2.0, https://github.com/whocov/nowcastr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {nowcastr: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method},   author = {Mathias Leroy and Finlay Campbell},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://github.com/whocov/nowcastr}, }

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: R package nowcasting non-cumulative chain-ladder method. nowcast_cl() returns object intermediary results plot_nc_input(option = "triangle") / plot(= "data", option = "triangle") plot_nc_input(option = "millipede") / plot(= "data", option = "millipede") plot_delays() / plot(= "delays") plot_nowcast() / plot(= "results") calculate_retro_score(): Calculate retro-scores groups rm_repeated_values(): Remove duplicated reported values reporting matrix fill_future_reported_values(): Fill future reported values last known values

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
code: devtools::install_github("whocov/nowcastr")

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
code: library(nowcastr)  # Run nowcast with built-in demo data result <- nowcast_demo %>%    nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )  # View results print(result@results) plot(result, which = "results")

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: Dataset least 2 date columns value column. dataset can also multiple group-columns batch processing. Note delays (difference 2 dates) constant intervals, .e., multiples 1 day 7 days.

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
code: ## Visualize input data nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )  ## Fill the missing (optional) data <-   nowcast_demo %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     max_delay = "auto"   )  ## Visualize the change data %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
code: nowcast <- data %>%   nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     do_model_fitting = TRUE,   )

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
code: print(nowcast@delays) nowcast %>% plot(which = "delays") print(nowcast@results) nowcast %>% plot(which = "results")

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: nowcast_cl() returns S7 object class nowcast_results following properties: Access properties $: Available methods: - print(nowcast) - Summary nowcast results - plot(nowcast, = "delays") - Delay distribution - plot(nowcast, = "results") - Nowcast time series
code: nowcast$delays nowcast$results nowcast$params

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: Summary: Input Data: Ensure three core columns: observed_value / date_of_reporting / date_of_occurrence (e.g. date_of_event / date_of_onset) Calculate reporting_delay (= date_of_reporting - date_of_occurrence) Compute completeness (= observed_value / true_value (approximated last_reported_value)) Aggregate avg_completeness reporting_delay Optional: Fit curve Apply Nowcast: nowcast = observed_value / avg_completeness

what: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
text: retro-score amount retro-adjustments / max possible retro-adjustments higher better nowcast_cl() retro_score = n_changes / max_changes = retro_adjustments / max_retro_adj Notes: "retro-adjustments" = "value changes" retro-score = number changes / number ywks (max changes)

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
code: calculate_retro_score(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting,   col_value,   group_cols = NULL,   method = "2D_allgroups",   max_delay = Inf,   aggrby )

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. method '2D_allgroups' (number changes 2D triangle) 'at_least_1_change_by_occ' (number occurrence dates least 2 reported values) max_delay Maximum delay consider. (works method '2D_allgroups') aggrby character vector column names aggregate .

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
text: tibble group cols + retro_score (percentage 0-1)

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
code: generate_test_data() %>%   calculate_retro_score(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = NULL     # , aggrby = country     # , method = "at_least_1_change_by_occ"   ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   n_changes max_retro_adj retro_score #>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1        72            72           1

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. max_delay Inf / 'auto' / NULL / integer. 'auto' NULL keep max_delay input.

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
text: data frame columns df, rows added missing reporting dates NA values col_value filled last available observation occurrence date within group.

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
code: library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  generate_test_data() %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = NULL   ) #> # A tibble: 126 × 4 #>    date_occurrence date_report delay value #>    <date>          <date>      <int> <dbl> #>  1 2025-02-09      2025-02-09      0  50   #>  2 2025-02-08      2025-02-09      1  79.7 #>  3 2025-02-08      2025-02-08      0  50   #>  4 2025-02-07      2025-02-09      2  91.7 #>  5 2025-02-07      2025-02-08      1  79.7 #>  6 2025-02-07      2025-02-07      0  50   #>  7 2025-02-06      2025-02-09      3  96.6 #>  8 2025-02-06      2025-02-08      2  91.7 #>  9 2025-02-06      2025-02-07      1  79.7 #> 10 2025-02-06      2025-02-06      0  50   #> # ℹ 116 more rows

what: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: Create synthetic long-format test data following asymptotic delay curve constant final value. Formula: value = final_value * (c + 1 * (1 - exp(-b * .data$delay))))

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
code: generate_test_data(   reportdate_from = "2025-02-01",   n_reportdates = 9,   delay_from = 0,   n_delays = 10,   time_units = "days",   final_value = 100,   c = 0.5,   b = 0.9,   remove_delay = FALSE )

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: reportdate_from Character Date. Start report date (e.g. "2025-02-01"). n_reportdates Integer. Number consecutive report dates generate. delay_from Integer >= 0. Minimum delay value. n_delays Integer. Number delay values generate. time_units Time units. Accepted values: ("auto", "secs", "mins", "hours", "days", "weeks"). final_value Numeric. Asymptotic target (formula). c Numeric (0-1). Baseline fraction final_value added curve. b Numeric. Rate constant controlling fast y approaches ; larger b → faster approach. remove_delay Logical. TRUE, remove delay column output.

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: tibble (long format) columns date_report (Date), date_occurrence (Date), delay (integer, optional) value (numeric).

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: defaults returns n_reportdates × (n_delays - delay_from + 1) rows (9 × 10 = 90). reportdate_from may provided Date parsable character; lubridate units used stepping.

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
text: Performs nowcasting using non-cumulative Chain-Ladder Method. Input dataset 2 date columns; 1 value column flexible number group columns. Output dataset latest reported data joined completeness ratio final value_predicted option use model-free completeness ratio calculation (faster) use model-fitted completeness (slower).

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
code: nowcast_cl(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting,   col_value,   group_cols = NULL,   time_units = "days",   max_delay = NULL,   max_reportunits = 10,   max_completeness = 5,   min_completeness_samples = 1,   use_weighted_method = TRUE,   do_propagate_missing_delays = FALSE,   do_model_fitting = TRUE,   model_names = c("monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz",     "asymptotic", "linear"),   do_use_modelled_completeness = TRUE,   rss_threshold = 0.01,   output = "all" )

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. time_units Time unit delay calculation. Accepted values: ("auto", "secs", "mins", "hours", "days", "weeks"). max_delay Max delay keep analysis. Interger NULL. NULL, take max delay data. max_reportunits Max number col_date_reporting use (unit time_units). max_completeness Maximum completeness ratio (e.g., 2 = 200%). min_completeness_samples Min number samples required calculate completeness. Integer 1 max_reportunits. use_weighted_method Use weighted method, linear weight older reported completeness values do_propagate_missing_delays Fill missing completeness lower delay value. do_model_fitting Fit model completeness delay. Models useful indicators like 'time 95% compl.' also soften variability. model_names Character vector names models test best fit. Accepted values: "monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz", "asymptotic", "linear" do_use_modelled_completeness Use modelled completeness values nowcasting. Boolean NULL (auto selective).  (unused do_model_fitting=FALSE) rss_threshold Minimum RSS threshold use model-fitted values. used do_use_modelled_completeness NULL. RSS fit higher observed completeness used nowcasting. output function return? 'nowcast' = data.frame / '' = S7 object

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
text: Either S7 object class nowcast_results; data.frame output="nowcast".

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
code: input <- generate_test_data() res <- input %>%   nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     time_units = "days"   ) ## plot results: # res %>% plot(which = "results")

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
text: Evaluates historical performance nowcast_cl() repeatedly peeling back recent reporting period comparing predictions eventually-reported true values (highest col_date_reporting per occurrence date).

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
code: nowcast_eval(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting,   col_value,   group_cols = NULL,   n_past = 10,   time_units = "days",   max_delay = NULL,   max_reportunits = 10,   max_completeness = 5,   min_completeness_samples = 1,   use_weighted_method = TRUE,   do_propagate_missing_delays = FALSE,   do_model_fitting = TRUE,   model_names = c("monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz",     "asymptotic", "linear"),   do_use_modelled_completeness = TRUE,   rss_threshold = 0.01 )

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. n_past Integer. Number past reporting periods evaluate. iteration peels one reporting period (time_units). time_units Time unit delay calculation. Accepted values: ("auto", "secs", "mins", "hours", "days", "weeks"). max_delay Max delay keep analysis. Interger NULL. NULL, take max delay data. max_reportunits Max number col_date_reporting use (unit time_units). max_completeness Maximum completeness ratio (e.g., 2 = 200%). min_completeness_samples Min number samples required calculate completeness. Integer 1 max_reportunits. use_weighted_method Use weighted method, linear weight older reported completeness values do_propagate_missing_delays Fill missing completeness lower delay value. do_model_fitting Fit model completeness delay. Models useful indicators like 'time 95% compl.' also soften variability. model_names Character vector names models test best fit. Accepted values: "monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz", "asymptotic", "linear" do_use_modelled_completeness Use modelled completeness values nowcasting. Boolean NULL (auto selective).  (unused do_model_fitting=FALSE) rss_threshold Minimum RSS threshold use model-fitted values. used do_use_modelled_completeness NULL. RSS fit higher observed completeness used nowcasting.

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
text: S7 object class nowcast_eval_results slots: detail data.frame per-prediction errors (one row per occurrence date x delay x past period). summary data.frame aggregated SMAPE proportion_pred_is_better, group delay. params list parameters used. n_past number past periods evaluated. time_start POSIXct start time. time_end POSIXct end time.

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
code: input <- generate_test_data() eval_res <- nowcast_eval(   df = input,   col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting = date_report,   col_value = value,   n_past = 10,   time_units = "days" ) #> Warning: n_past (10) exceeds available reporting periods (8). Using all available.

what: S7 object class for nowcast_eval() Results — nowcast_eval_results
title: S7 object class for nowcast_eval() Results — nowcast_eval_results
text: nowcast_eval() function returns object class.

title: S7 object class for nowcast_eval() Results — nowcast_eval_results
code: nowcast_eval_results(   detail = (function (.data = list(), row.names = NULL)   {      if (is.null(row.names))     {          list2DF(.data)      }      else {          out <- list2DF(.data,     length(row.names)) attr(out, "row.names") <- row.names          out      }      })(),   summary = (function (.data = list(), row.names = NULL)   {      if (is.null(row.names))     {          list2DF(.data)      }      else {          out <- list2DF(.data,     length(row.names)) attr(out, "row.names") <- row.names          out      }      })(),   params = list(),   n_past = integer(0),   time_start = (function (.data = double(), tz = "")   {      .POSIXct(.data, tz = tz)      })(),   time_end = (function (.data = double(), tz = "")   {      .POSIXct(.data, tz = tz)      })() )

title: S7 object class for nowcast_eval() Results — nowcast_eval_results
text: detail data.frame. Per-prediction errors columns observed value, predicted value, true value, SAPE (pred obs), pred_is_better. summary data.frame. Aggregated metrics per group x delay: SMAPE (pred obs), SMAPE improvement, proportion_pred_is_better, Wilson CIs. params list. Parameters used evaluation run. n_past numeric. Number past reporting periods evaluated. time_start POSIXct. Time function started. time_end POSIXct. Time function ended.

what: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
title: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
text: nowcast_cl function returns object class.

title: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
code: nowcast_results(name, params, time_start, time_end, n_groups, max_delay,   data, completeness, delays, models, results)

title: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
text: name character string timestamp run. params list parameters used nowcasting (unevaluated call). time_start sys time function started. time_end sys time function ended. n_groups number groups processed. max_delay maximum delay used. data Dataframe. original input data frame (required columns). completeness Dataframe. original input data frame delays completeness columns. delays Dataframe. data frame final aggregated completeness estimates per delay (+ modelled column do_model_fitting TRUE). models Dataframe. resulting fitted models (empty data frame do_model_fitting FALSE) results Dataframe. data frame resulting nowcasting predictions.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Results — plot_nowcast_eval
text: Plots horizontal bar chart nowcasting evaluation metrics per group, selected delay. Two panels shown side side: SMAPE improvement: median per-prediction SMAPE difference (obs minus pred; positive = prediction better), IQR error bar. Proportion better: share past periods prediction beat raw observed, centered 0 (0.5 = improvement), Wilson 95% CI. Bars coloured whether improvement significant (IQR / CI fully zero) .

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Results — plot_nowcast_eval
code: plot_nowcast_eval(   x,   delay = NULL,   color_good = "dodgerblue1",   color_bad = "firebrick1",   alpha_less = 0.35,   ... )

title: Remove duplicated reported values in reporting matrix — rm_repeated_values
code: library(dplyr) generate_test_data(n_delays = 20, n_reportdates = 20) %>%   mutate(value = round(value, 1)) %>% ## make values identical   rm_repeated_values(     col_value = value,     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report   ) %>%   plot_triangle(     col_value = value,     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report   )
